title: [$250] Empty (0-byte) receipt files can be uploaded from web, causing a server error

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Melvin Triage
labels.description: Automatic AI Analysis of an issue

body:
## Problem When a user submits an expense with a receipt on web, if the attached file is a 0-byte `Blob` (e.g., from a failed camera capture or an uninitialized `canvas.toBlob()`), the client sends it to the server, which then throws a `666` error: "There was a problem creating the receipt." The web `isFileUploadable` check only validates `file instanceof Blob` and does not verify the blob has any content. This lets empty blobs pass through and reach the API, where `filesize()` returns 0 and the request fails. Related server error: [Expensify/Expensify#610852](https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/610852) ([comment](https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/610852#issuecomment-4114929607)) ## Solution Add a `Blob.size > 0` guard on web so empty files are rejected client-side before the API request is made. - In `src/libs/isFileUploadable/index.ts`, add a size check so `isFileUploadable` returns `false` for zero-byte blobs - Consider also adding a size check on native (`index.native.ts`) if applicable - Surface a user-facing error message when a receipt file is empty rather than letting it silently fail on the server
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @eVoloshchak

Create new account & workspace 2. Categories, Tags, and Tax Rates are configured/enabled for the account Steps 1. Navigate to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Navigate to Accounts > Expense rules 3. Select New Rule and Configure it as follows: If the expense contains: Merchant name as: Test Apply these updates Merchant to: New Test Apply these updates Category to: "Travel" Apply these updates Tag to: Any tag Apply these updates Description to: Any description Change Reimbursability to: Non-reimbursable Change Billable to: Billable Add to a report named: Expense Rule Report Select 'Create report if necessary' 4. Click "Save rule" 5. Go to workspace chat or tap FAB 6. Create an expense with the merchant set to 'Testing' and only setting the merchant, date, and amount 7. Open expense preview & observe "Billable no longer valid" violation shown ## Expected Result: Billable no longer valid violation should not be displayed in expense preview when personal set rule to billable ## Actual Result: Billable no longer valid violation shown in expense preview when personal rule set to Billable ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/20ec6bb6-181b-4354-abed-75da5d50ddee [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Allow configuring Itemized Receipt requirement via Spreadsheet upload as well as manual

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.42-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/84985 **Issue reported by:** **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#expensify-open-source](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C01GTK53T8Q/p1770826705524259) ## Action Performed: 1. Navigate to Workspace settings > categories, where category rules can be configured 2. Set **Receipts** to **Not required** for a single category 3. Confirm that **Itemized Receipts** is automatically set to **Not required** for manually configured categories 4. Prepare a spreadsheet of categories and set one category to "not_required" for Receipts Required 5. Upload the spreadsheet into the workspace 6. Review the imported category settings ## Description: When configuring categories manually, setting **Receipts** to **Not required** correctly forces **Itemized Receipts** (`maxAmountNoItemizedReceipt`) to **Not required**. However, this logic is not applied when categories are created or updated via spreadsheet upload. This leads to inconsistent configurations where **Receipts** is **Not required**, but **Itemized Receipts** is not aligned and still set as "default" Additionally, the spreadsheet import flow does not currently support explicitly setting the `maxAmountNoItemizedReceipt` field, which prevents admins from defining this behavior directly in the upload. The spreadsheet import should: 1. Apply the same normalization logic as the manual UI flow 2. Support a `maxAmountNoItemizedReceipt` column with `required` / `not_required` values ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a categories spreadsheet for import 2. Set categories with **Receipts = Not required** 3. Upload the spreadsheet 4. Open workspace category settings 5. Check the **Itemized Receipts** values ## Expected Result: - Spreadsheet-imported categories follow the same logic as manual configuration - If **Receipts = Not required**, then **Itemized Receipts = Not required** - The spreadsheet supports a `maxAmountNoItemizedReceipt` field with `required` / `not_required` values - Imported data is consistent and predictable ## Actual Result: - Spreadsheet-imported categories do not apply the normalization logic - **Itemized Receipts** may remain incorrectly configured - No support exists for defining `maxAmountNoItemizedReceipt` in the spreadsheet ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Add updated automatic/scan flow to native share sheet creation flow

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
We recently added this page to the core Create Expense > Scan flow, which makes it possible to not only enter the description, but also change the category, tag, report, and whatever other expense details are enabled on the workspace. That said, if you're on mobile and you do the following: - Tap on a receipt image on your camera row - Tap the `Share` option - Choose `Expensify` in the option list Then we show a completely different screen with less features. **In this issue, we need to make sure that the updated scan flow options are included in the native share sheet flow, such that the experience in both flows is exactly the same.**
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @hoangzinh
